note_data.title: Kinematics of Linear Motion
keyPoints: Kinematics deals with the motion of objects without considering the forces that cause the motion
Distance is a scalar quantity while displacement is a vector quantity
Speed is the rate of change of distance while velocity is the rate of change of displacement
Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with respect to time
Three equations of motion: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at², v² = u² + 2as
Uniform motion means constant velocity (zero acceleration)
Non-uniform motion means changing velocity (non-zero acceleration)
Free fall is motion under gravity with acceleration g = 9.8 m/s²
Distance-time and velocity-time graphs help analyze motion patterns

definitions.definition: The branch of mechanics which deals with the motion of objects without considering the forces that cause the motion
The shortest distance between initial and final positions of a moving object in a specific direction
The rate of change of displacement with respect to time
The rate of change of velocity with respect to time
Motion in which an object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time

importantQuestions.question: Differentiate between distance and displacement.
importantQuestions.answer: Distance is the total path length covered by an object (scalar). Displacement is the shortest distance between initial and final positions in a specific direction (vector).

importantQuestions.question: Derive the equation v = u + at from first principles.
importantQuestions.answer: Let initial velocity = u, final velocity = v, acceleration = a, time = t. By definition, acceleration = (final velocity - initial velocity)/time. Therefore, a = (v - u)/t. Cross multiplying: at = v - u. Rearranging: v = u + at. This is the first equation of motion.

mcqs.question: A car traveling from Karachi to Hyderabad covers 150 km in 2 hours. Its average speed is:
Which of the following represents uniform motion?
The acceleration due to gravity is:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse distance with displacement - remember displacement can be zero even if distance is not
Always write the formula before solving numerical problems
Mixing up speed and velocity - speed is scalar, velocity is vector
Forgetting to mention direction when dealing with vector quantities
Not converting units properly (km/h to m/s)
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long derivation question in every BSEK paper
Memorize all three equations of motion - derivations are frequently asked
Practice drawing distance-time and velocity-time graphs
Always show complete working in numerical problems
formulas: Average speed = Total distance / Total time
Average velocity = Total displacement / Total time
note_data.keyDiagram: Distance-time graph showing uniform motion (straight line) and non-uniform motion (curved line). Velocity-time graph showing constant velocity (horizontal line) and uniform acceleration (straight sloped line). Students must label axes clearly with units.
